rationale: The subject home is inside an orange bounding box spanning approximately 255 px (W) × 268 px (H), equal to ~54.1 ft × 56.8 ft. The house is rotated ~35° off north. The roof has two identifiable sections: (1) a primary multi-plane hip-roofed section occupying the northern ~60% of the box (~210 × 175 px, 82% fill ≈ 1,357 sq ft) and (2) a lighter-toned attached addition or flat-roof section in the southern ~40% (~175 × 110 px, 88% fill ≈ 763 sq ft). Combined polygon estimate: ~2,120 sq ft; bounding-box × 72% fill gives ~2,213 sq ft — within 4%, so ~2,150 sq ft adopted. Edge estimates: two ridge sections totaling ~155 px (~33 ft); six hip lines averaging ~80 px each (~102 ft total); one to two valley lines where sections meet (~90 px, ~19 ft); minimal rake edges given hip construction (~25 px, ~5 ft); and perimeter eave lines (~780 px, ~165 ft). Confidence is moderate due to the diagonal roof orientation, possible active construction on the southern section, and shadow obscuring some lower eave lines.
